text:
In a quiet forest, a wise old owl perched on an oak.

"A golden sunset," he muttered softly.

A flash of crimson darted through the sky.

A wise old squirrel zipped past.

"Well, don't holler," chirped the squirrel. 

The owl fluttered his wings delicately.

The squirrel paused to listen.

"What's that? An echo?"

"In the wind!"

"A curious thing!"

Later—

A wise old pinecone rolled towards them.

"The stars sing."

The squirrel looked up.

"It's our moon?"

"No…"

"It twinkles! Just look!"


The squirrel hooted cheerfully.
